title: Microsoft announces layoffs of 3,200 Xbox employees and sale of several studios
summary: The company calls the restructuring the most significant in Xbox history, aiming to focus on key franchises.

title: Nintendo will stop selling the original Switch in Europe as of February 2027
summary: The Japanese company plans to discontinue the entire Switch line (standard, Lite, and OLED) in the region to focus on the new generation.

title: Xbox layoffs severely affect The Elder Scrolls Online team
summary: The developer studio loses half of its staff and is forced to delay and modify the game's roadmap.

title: The industry debates the future of physical discs amid the advance of digital
summary: Groups, developers, and politicians defend the physical format while companies move away from it for economic reasons.

title: Xbox Game Pass loses subscribers and fails to meet targets
summary: The subscription platform has fallen to 30 million subscribers, far below Microsoft's expectations.

title: Xbox sets goal of entertaining over one billion people per day
summary: Xbox management sets a goal considered unrealistic by analysts and media, amid the restructuring.

title: Bethesda and ZeniMax will focus efforts on major franchises like Fallout and The Elder Scrolls
summary: Xbox's restructuring prioritizes ZeniMax's most successful franchises, though it does not rule out others like Doom or Quake.

title: The future of Arkane and Marvel's Blade is uncertain after Xbox restructuring
summary: Arkane's founder jokes about buying the studio, while Blade's development is in danger and the team is temporarily saved by French law.

title: Reactions to Xbox mass layoffs range from outrage to resignation
summary: Analysts, former employees, and the gaming community criticize the company's management and warn of the strain on the industry.

title: Developers ask to review Steam refund policies due to player abuse
summary: A short game has been refunded 55,000 times, sparking debate on minimum length and viability of small titles.

title: A plague outbreak in World of Warcraft spirals out of control and wreaks havoc among players
summary: An old bug that caused a virtual pandemic has had a new episode reminiscent of the original 2005 incident.

title: Tekken's Katsuhiro Harada defends judging games by sales success and compares Sakurai to a Saiyan
summary: The veteran producer offers various opinions on the industry, game valuation, and fellow developers.

title: Bethesda Game Studios and ZeniMax also suffer significant staff cuts
summary: Xbox layoffs continue to impact multiple company studios, including id Software and ZeniMax Online.

title: Compulsion Games and Double Fine regain independence after Xbox reorganization
summary: Both studios leave the Xbox structure and become independent developers again, keeping their games.

title: Nintendo will launch a Switch 2 revision with removable battery in Europe
summary: The new hardware version will allow replacing the battery without tools, though it will not be available in the United States.

title: Microsoft confirms that Halo: Campaign Evolved will be released on physical disc
summary: The company positions itself in favor of physical format amid controversy over the removal of discs on PlayStation.

title: The feared 'red line of death' of Steam Machine turns out to be less serious than it seems
summary: Several users reported a red line on the screen that turned out to be a reversible, albeit unsettling, error.

title: Jaleco Arcade Collection coming to PS5, Switch, and PC this winter
summary: Two volumes of Jaleco arcade classics will be released on modern platforms.

title: First reactions to Cyberpunk Edgerunners season 2 are very positive
summary: The new season of the animated series continues to receive praise from critics and audiences.

title: Obsidian Entertainment loses a quarter of its staff in Xbox cuts
summary: The studio behind The Outer Worlds and Grounded suffers mass layoffs affecting even veterans with decades of service.
